note:
苹果公司今天宣布了一款用于第八代iPad的新CPU，A12仿生。IPad基本款配备的A12 Bionic提供了比第七代iPad快40%的CPU和20%的GPU，苹果表示，这也意味着A12的速度是最畅销的Windows笔记本电脑的两倍，比最畅销的Android平板电脑快三倍，比领先的Chromebook快六倍。
正如许多分析师和泄密者预测的那样，苹果在今天的直播活动中推出了iPad Air。新的iPad Air比上次更新晚了大约一年半，但它比之前的更新做了更多的事情：它彻底改变了设备的整体设计。IPad Air将于10月份上市，起价599美元。它有五种颜色：银色、太空灰、玫瑰金、绿色和天蓝色。
受更昂贵的iPad Pro的启发，iPad Air现在大大减少了边框，没有主页按钮，屏幕边缘变圆。然而，它没有iPad Pro用于面部ID身份验证的前置TrueDepth传感器阵列。相反，它为苹果设备带来了一些新东西-尽管对于一般的消费移动设备来说并不新鲜。苹果设计了一个新版本的触控ID，集成到顶部按钮中(在视觉上看起来没有什么不同)。按钮下面是一个激光切割的蓝宝石镜头和用于读取用户指纹的定制传感器。
IPad Air搭载了全新的A14仿生CPU，采用5纳米制程技术。它是一款六核CPU，配有两个高性能内核和四个功耗更低、效率更高的内核，用于执行更简单的后台任务。与前几代产品相比，A14 Bionic提供了30%的GPU性能提升，苹果公司表示，它的图形性能是典型笔记本电脑的两倍。
它还提供了一个16核的神经引擎来提高机器学习性能-可以包括图形编辑器、语音助手等中使用的许多功能-以及一个新的DJ应用程序，允许在iPad上进行空中抓取手势。
IPad Air还将带来一个USB-C连接器，允许连接到外部摄像头等设备，以及速度更快60%的LTE调制解调器。摄像头已更新至1200万像素，支持4K视频和增强的视频稳定性。扬声器系统也进行了重新设计，立体声传输采用横向模式。
同样在设计领域，iPad Air现在有一个接口，与该公司几个月前为iPad Pro推出的Magic Keyboard外围设备兼容。该外围设备提供触摸板(iPadOS去年增加了对触摸板的支持)，键盘类型与苹果同名桌面键盘大致相同。魔术键盘售价279美元，iPad Air智能键盘对开本售价179美元。
note_en:
Apple announced a new CPU today for the  eighth-generation iPad, the A12 Bionic. The A12 Bionic, featured in the base iPad model, offers a 40 percent faster CPU and 20 percent faster GPU than seventh-generation iPads, and Apple says that also means the A12 is twice as fast as the top-selling Windows laptop, three times faster than the top-selling Android tablet, and six times faster than the leading Chromebook.
     Just as many analysts and leakers predicted, Apple  introduced an iPad Air during its "Time Flies" livestream event today. The new iPad Air comes about a year and a half after the last refresh, but it does more than the previous update did: it overhauls the overall design of the device. The iPad Air will be available in October,  starting at $599. It comes in five colors: silver, space gray, rose gold, green, and sky blue.
 Taking cues from the more expensive iPad Pro, the iPad Air now has drastically reduced bezels, no home button, and rounded screen edges. It does not, however, have the front-facing TrueDepth sensor array that the iPad Pro uses for Face ID authentication. Rather, it introduces something new to Apple devices—albeit not new to consumer mobile devices in general. Apple designed a new version of touch ID, integrated into the top button (which does not appear visually different). Underneath the button is a laser-cut sapphire lens and custom sensor for reading users' fingerprints.
 The iPad Air gets the new A14 Bionic CPU, built on 5nm process technology. It's a six-core CPU with two high-performance cores and four lower-power, more efficient cores for simpler background tasks. The A14 Bionic offers a 30 percent GPU performance boost compared to previous generations, and Apple says it puts up double the graphics performance of typical laptops.
 It also offers a 16-core neural engine for machine-learning performance—which can include many features used in graphics editors, voice assistants, and more—as well as a new DJ application which allows "air scratching" gestures made above the iPad.
    The iPad Air will also bring a USB-C connector, allowing connections to devices such as external cameras, and a 60 percent faster LTE modem. The camera has been updated to 12MP with 4K video and enhanced video stabilization. The speaker system is also redesigned, with stereo delivery in landscape mode.
  Also in the realm of design, the iPad Air now has a connector compatible with the Magic Keyboard peripheral the company launched for the iPad Pro a few months ago. That peripheral offers a trackpad (trackpad support was added to iPadOS last year) and roughly the same type of keyboard found in Apple's desktop keyboard of the same name. The Magic Keyboard will cost $279, and Smart Keyboard Folio for iPad Air will be $179.
title: 新款iPad Air全屏，增加魔术键盘支持
title_en: The new iPad Air goes all-screen, adds Magic Keyboard support

note:
迪士尼放弃了乔恩·法夫罗(Jon Favreau)创作的获得艾美奖提名的首部星球大战真人电视连续剧《曼达洛人》(The Mandalorian)第二季的第一部预告片。这部电视剧由佩德罗·帕斯卡(Pedro Pascal)饰演名义上的曼达洛人丁·迪加林(Din Djarin)，故事发生在帝国灭亡几年后、第一教团出现之前。
基本的前提是，在《绝地归来》中击败帝国之后，有一段混乱和无法无天的时期，新政府挣扎着从废墟中走出来。帕斯卡的赏金猎人是银河系外围的一名孤独的枪手，远离新共和国的权威。
在S1中，曼达洛人正在寻找一个赏金猎人协会的领导人格里夫·卡尔加(卡尔·韦瑟斯饰)支付更多的奖金。卡尔加把他带到维尔纳·赫尔佐格的客户那里，他是一个无名的人物，雇佣曼达洛人来取回一笔有价值的资产。事实证明，这就是“孩子”(又名“尤达宝宝”，由木偶师饰演)，他成了即刻爆发的明星。整个互联网(显然还有赫尔佐格)爱上了“尤达宝宝”(虽然他不是尤达宝宝，不过他只是一个同种族的婴儿--这让他大约50岁)。
曼达洛人埋藏已久的利他主义在他意识到客户的意图是伤害(甚至可能是杀死)孩子的时候出现了，他转而带着孩子潜逃了。这也让他的脑袋受到了赏金，第一季的大部分时间都是在这对不太可能的二人组之后进行的，因为这位曼达洛人试图为这个孩子找到一个安全的避风港。他们的主要追求者：前帝国总督莫夫·吉迪恩(Giancarlo Esposito)。幸运的是，他们在几个地方找到了有用的盟友，还有来自被摧毁的奥德兰星球的前起义军突击队员卡拉·沙恩(吉娜·卡拉诺饰)。事实证明，这个孩子自己也有一些令人惊讶的能力。
正如萨姆·马奇科维奇(Ars&39；Sam Machkovech)在去年对该剧试播集的评论中指出的那样，对于一代影迷来说，“曼达洛人”是“星球大战”，他们记得这部系列电影是一种美学、一种精神气质，以及在道德上混合发现的承诺。他特别赞扬了实际效果、精心塑造的外星人服装以及故意制造的奶酪球CGI，并添加了……。考虑到华丽的布景设计，约翰·威廉姆斯(John Williams)级别的全新作曲配乐，以及一群享受停顿、节拍和紧张时刻(无论是动作还是喜剧)的演员阵容，这些短暂的CGI时刻都是可以原谅的。
这部电视剧凭借八集的新生郊游获得了15项艾美奖提名。而且它很容易就在我们2019年的顶级电视节目中占有一席之地。马切科维奇写道，事实证明，法夫罗的创意是真人版“星球大战”中最像“星际迷航”一样讲故事的杀手级工具。更好的是，它本周的反常和全新的星球进程与一个恰当的武士故事配对，就像宗教的、狂热的和冲突的曼多本人所支撑的那样。
所以很自然，我们都渴望S2(据说第三季的前期制作已经开始了)。卡拉诺，天气和埃斯波西托都将在第二季回归，后者在赛季决赛中被卡尔加和曼达洛人击落而幸存下来。唉，我们告别了S1中的几个伟大人物，包括刺客芬内克·尚德(文明娜饰)；库伊尔(尼克·诺尔特配音，与戴着动漫面罩的米斯蒂·罗萨斯配音)，一位乌格诺特湿润农场主和前帝国奴隶，他牺牲了自己的生命来保护孩子；以及机器人IG-11(由泰卡·怀蒂蒂配音)。
新的S2演员包括罗萨里奥·道森(Rosario Dawson)饰演阿索卡·塔诺(Ahsoka Tano)，阿纳金·天行者(Anakin Skywalker)的前绝地学徒，现在是起义军联盟的间谍头目；凯蒂·萨克霍夫(Katee Sackhoff)饰演波-卡坦·克里兹(Bo-Katan Kryze)(重新出演她在克隆人战争和起义军动画片中的配音角色)，他领导着一个名为夜猫子的曼达洛人团体；蒂莫西·奥利芬特(Timothy Olyphant)饰演科布·范斯(Cobb Vanth)，一个
S2的官方前提相当模糊：“曼达洛人”和“孩子”继续他们的旅程，在银河帝国崩溃后的动荡时代，面对敌人和集结的盟友，穿过一个危险的银河系。预告片没有提供更多的东西。曼多接到指示，要让这个孩子和他自己的同类团聚，尽管那个物种可能潜伏在哪里显然是他必须自己弄清楚的事情。它将包括寻找一个被称为绝地的巫师团体，他们是曼达洛人在亿万年前的不共戴天的敌人。我们看到了太空战、风暴部队、我们的二人组在遥远的冰冷世界里漫步的场景，当然，还有许许多多的尤达宝宝(Baby Yoda)。曼达洛人仍然是一个令人敬畏的对手，正如一群暴徒认识到的那样，这对它不利。
曼达洛人的NS2将于2020年10月30日在迪士尼+上首次亮相。下面是一些额外的宣传剧照。
title: “就是这样”：迪士尼推出了曼达洛人S2的第一个预告片
title_en: “This is the way”: Disney drops first trailer for The Mandalorian S2

note:
唐纳德·特朗普总统花了几个月的时间推动TikTok被禁止或出售给一家美国公司。他似乎已经如愿以偿，甲骨文证实它与ByteDance就TikTok达成了一项协议。然而，这笔交易并不一定能缓解白宫对这款广受欢迎的视频应用程序的担忧，而且在短时间内，这笔交易距离完成还有很长的路要走。
协议的具体条款仍未公开。这一安排并不是特朗普上周五推动的全面销售。中国对机器学习和人工智能算法的出口禁令阻止了这种直接收购。
甲骨文对这笔交易几乎没有发表什么言论，这笔交易最初是在周日晚些时候泄露的。周一上午，该公司证实，它已在9月12日至13日的周末向财政部提交了一份成为ByteDance值得信赖的技术提供商的提案，以供审查。周二上午，作为提交给美国证券交易委员会(SEC)的文件的一部分，该公司重复了这一声明。
周一，美国财政部长史蒂文·姆努钦(Steven Mnuchin)与CNBC就这笔交易的下一步进行了交谈。姆努钦没有讨论这笔交易的细节，但重申甲骨文是值得信赖的技术合作伙伴。他补充说，甲骨文就国家安全问题进行了多次陈述，并承诺将创建TikTok Global，作为一家总部位于美国的公司，拥有2万个新工作岗位。
该提案已提交财政部，因为美国外国投资委员会(CFIUS)就设在那里。CFIUS由来自十几个不同机构的成员组成，对外国企业收购部分或全部美国企业的交易进行国家安全审查。拟议中的甲骨文交易在某种程度上是相反的-一家美国公司正在投资一个外国平台-通常可能不会经过美国外国投资委员会的审查。然而，到目前为止，该委员会一直深度参与了整个TikTok事件。
去年秋天，美国外国投资委员会开始对TikTok进行追溯性审查，TikTok是在ByteDance收购美国公司Musical.ly并重新命名后成立的。白宫于8月14日以一项行政命令(PDF)结束了这一审查，宣布对Musical.ly的收购确实是对国家安全的威胁，并追溯禁止了这项收购，并要求CFIUS监督某种撤资过程。
姆努钦告诉CNBC，截止日期是9月20日，尽管特朗普多次宣布截止日期是9月15日。从现在到那时，我们正在经历两个过程。一个是美国外国投资委员会的审查，另一个是总统行政命令下的国家安全审查…。本周，我们将在CFIUS委员会上审查这一点，然后我们将向总统提出建议，并与他一起审查。
当被具体问及TikTok的专有算法对国家安全构成的威胁时，姆努钦没有直接回答这个问题。毕竟，TikTok的专有算法最终可能不会成为交易的一部分。相反，他说，从我们的立场来看，我们需要确保代码是安全的，美国人的数据是安全的，手机是安全的，我们将在接下来的几天里与我们的技术团队寻求与甲骨文进行讨论。
据《华尔街日报》报道，特朗普的顾问们对于政府是应该接受甲骨文交易，还是应该采取更严厉的行动意见不一。根据华尔街日报，姆钦支持甲骨文的交易，并在周末之前获得了商务部长威尔伯·罗斯(Wilbur Ross)的支持。“华尔街日报”没有点名白宫内部据报道反对这笔交易的对华鹰派人士。然而，白宫外的共和党人公开要求CFIUS禁止这笔交易。
经常就技术问题大声疾呼的密苏里州共和党参议员乔希·霍利(Josh Hawley)敦促监管机构阻止这笔交易。
现有证据只能得出一个结论：ByteDance无意放弃对TikTok的最终控制权，霍利在给姆努钦的一封信中写道(PDF)。
霍利写道，这笔交易是完全不可接受的，与总统8月6日的行政命令完全不一致，结论是全面出售或彻底禁止更可取。CFIUS应该立即拒绝任何甲骨文与ByteDance的合作，并将球送回ByteDance的球场，这样公司才能拿出更可接受的解决方案。
然而，消息人士今天上午告诉CNBC，白宫希望加快审批程序，最快可能在今天宣布为这笔交易开绿灯。
title: 甲骨文TikTok非收购寻求财政部和白宫批准
title_en: Oracle’s TikTok non-acquisition seeks Treasury, White House approval

note:
在美国卫生与公众服务部(Department Of Health And Human Services)发布的一个令人震惊的播客中，该部门的两名高级官员一再淡化新冠肺炎疫情，抨击缓解措施，称关闭面对面教育是“无稽之谈”，并表示美国记者“对公共卫生信息不屑一顾”。
该播客于9月11日在HHS网站上发布，是目前担任HHS公共事务助理部长的迈克尔·卡普托(Michael Caputo)主持的系列节目的一部分。虽然卡普托没有医疗保健方面的背景，但白宫在4月份将他任命为该部门的成员-据报道，此举是为了让白宫对HHS部长亚历克斯·阿扎尔(Alex Azar)有更多的控制。卡普托是特朗普的长期忠诚者，也是前竞选官员。他最初是罗杰·斯通(Roger Stone)的门徒，后来在莫斯科担任鲍里斯·叶利钦(Boris Yeltsin)的顾问，并为弗拉基米尔·普京(Vladimir Putin)做公关工作。
卡普托最近因干扰和改变疾病控制和预防中心研究人员准备的关于新冠肺炎的科学报告而登上头条。这种干预的目的是让报道更符合特朗普的信息，特朗普承认淡化了这场流行病。卡普托还在Facebook上发布了一段直播视频，“纽约时报”周一报道了这段视频。在视频中，卡普托在没有证据的情况下指责政府科学家从事“煽动”活动，并声称疾控中心窝藏着一支“抵抗部队”。他还谈到了他在华盛顿的公寓里的长长的“阴影”，并说左翼“杀手小队”正在为选举后的武装叛乱做准备。
通过他稍微乐观的HHS播客系列“学习曲线”，卡普托突出了HHS官员的工作，这样听众就可以“向我学习的人学习”。在9·11事件中-他特别称政府科学家是“一群令人难以置信的专家”-卡普托与HHS下属的药物滥用和精神健康服务管理局(SAMHSA)局长埃莉诺·麦坎斯-卡茨(Elinore McCance-Katz)进行了交谈。
McCance-Katz被卡普托描述为“该部门的天使之一”，他是一名精神病学家，拥有耶鲁大学传染病流行病学博士学位。她曾在奥巴马政府期间担任SAMHSA的首席医疗官，但在任职两年后辞职，称SAMHSA在治疗严重精神疾病方面做得不够。特朗普政府于2017年重新任命她为该机构的成员，自那以后，她公开表示支持特朗普对这场流行病的一些观点。
在播客中，卡普托和麦坎斯-卡茨的对话开始得不起眼，两人讨论了阿片类药物的流行和相关的HHS努力。但在长达一小时的节目播出约20分钟后，讨论转向了对在家工作命令和其他缓解措施的批评。卡普托和麦坎斯-卡茨都表示，居家命令是不必要的，只会加剧大流行对美国人的精神伤害。
虽然公共卫生专家早就注意到封锁确实是严厉的，但他们也承认，为了遏制新型冠状病毒SARS-CoV-2的潜伏传播，封锁是必要的。这样的行动限制和疏远措施在很大程度上有效地控制了世界各国的疫情-当然，除了美国，美国在控制这场致命的大流行方面严重失败。
尽管如此，卡普托和麦坎斯-卡茨对缓解策略不屑一顾，称其只由富人推动，他们可以很容易地留在家里。“那些说‘在家更安全’的人。呆在家里，往往是相当富裕的人，“卡普托说。
“是的，对他们来说，在家里可能更安全，”麦坎斯-卡茨回应说。“他们去了一些漂亮的房子，一些设施齐全的大房子。”
他们接着提出，像去看足球比赛或电影院这样的活动主要是“我们经济体系中较低阶层的人”喜欢的，这就是为什么这些活动受到限制的原因。与此同时，“有钱人的房子在海滩上，他们正在观看Netflix和其他所有流媒体平台，”卡普托补充道。他说，像那些在医院工作的人这样的基本工作者“无法获得这些订阅”。
“我要，我要说出来，”麦坎斯-卡茨不久之后说。“在我看来，在病毒有机会在全国传播之前，我们关闭了整个国家。为什么？“。她补充说，居家订单类似于“在我认为我们需要手术刀的时候使用大锤”。
卡普托同意了，他说：“毫无疑问。你知道吗？对我来说，损害已经造成。“。
两人接着讨论了大流行对儿童的影响，对许多地方暂停面对面学习以试图遏制疾病传播表示厌恶。“不知道怎么回学校就不安全，这是什么胡说八道？”McCance-Katz在注意到大多数感染SARS-CoV-2的儿童不会得重病后问道。尽管在他们注意到“下层”的人通常居住在更拥挤的、多代人的家庭之前，他们没有讨论儿童将病毒传染给他们社区的家庭成员和其他人的风险。
正如所指出的，卡普托一直在干预疾控中心关于大流行的科学报告，他接着指责美国媒体“不诚实”，扭曲了公众对该病毒的看法。他还指责媒体“敲打总统”，“践踏”了对新冠肺炎治疗和疫苗的乐观情绪。他说：“我不认为美国媒体会在乎公共卫生信息。”
接近尾声时，麦坎斯-卡茨提供了一个光明的前景。尽管她推测“数千”人可能会死于与流行病相关的绝望，但她认为美国人是有韧性的，“绝望情绪将会减弱”。
然而，卡普托以更阴暗、令人困惑的音符结束。尽管他在这一集的大部分时间里都在淡化这场大流行，但他在结束时强调了新冠肺炎的严重性和死亡人数。“我每天早上发现自己，第一次用我的声音，我就在谈论死亡，”他说。
“我们在会议中听到报告……。这些人(和新冠肺炎)，他们淹死在自己的液体里，“他说。“医生告诉我，这是一个人能有的最深的恐惧。他们临终时脸上的表情是他们永远不能忘记的。这些医生不能忘记这一点。而方式--也就是我说的--这是我早上要谈的第一件事。“。
根据Politico的一份最新报道，卡普托周二召开了一次紧急员工会议，就他在Facebook直播视频上的言论向HHS员工道歉，他在视频中声称疾控中心窝藏着一个“抵抗部队”。一些HHS工作人员告诉Politico，他们得到的印象是卡普托计划辞职。
到目前为止，美国已报告了650多万例新冠肺炎病例，19.5万多人死亡。
note_en:
In a stunning podcast released by the Department of Health and Human Services, two top officials at the department repeatedly downplayed the COVID-19 pandemic, railed against mitigation efforts, called closures of in-person schooling “nonsense,” and said US journalists do not “[give] a damn about public health information.”
 The podcast,  released on the HHS website September 11, is part of a series hosted by Michael Caputo, who currently holds the title of HHS assistant secretary of public affairs. Though Caputo has no background in health care, the White House installed him in the department in April—a move reportedly made to  assert more White House control over HHS Secretary Alex Azar. Caputo is a longtime Trump loyalist and former campaign official. He got his start as a protégé of Roger Stone and later worked as a Moscow-based advisor to Boris Yeltsin and did public relations work for Vladimir Putin.
  Caputo has most recently made headlines for working to  interfere with and alter scientific reports on COVID-19 prepared by researchers at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. The meddling was intended to make reports more in line with messaging from Trump, who has  admitted to downplaying the pandemic. Caputo also raised eyebrows with a  Facebook live video, reported by The New York Times Monday, in which, without evidence, he accused government scientists of engaging in “sedition” and claimed that the CDC is harboring a “resistance unit.” He also spoke of long “shadows” in his DC apartment and said left-wing “hit-squads” were preparing for armed insurrection after the election.
 With his slightly more upbeat HHS podcast series, The Learning Curve, Caputo spotlights the work of HHS officials so that listeners can “learn from the people that I am learning from.” In the September 11 episode—in which he notably calls government scientists “an incredible group of experts”—Caputo spoke with Elinore McCance-Katz, head of the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA), which is a branch of the HHS.
 McCance-Katz, who Caputo described as “one of the angels of the department,” is a psychiatrist and holds a PhD from Yale in infectious disease epidemiology. She was the chief medical officer of SAMHSA during the Obama administration but resigned after two years saying SAMHSA  wasn’t doing enough to treat people with serious mental illnesses. The Trump administration reappointed her to the agency in 2017, and she has since  publicly aligned with some of Trump’s views on the pandemic.
 In the podcast, Caputo and McCance-Katz’s conversation began unremarkably, with the two discussing the opioid epidemic and related HHS efforts. But about 20 minutes into the hour-long episode, the discussion shifted to criticism of stay-at-home orders and other mitigation efforts. Both Caputo and McCance-Katz suggested that stay-at-home orders were unnecessary and only exacerbated the mental toll the pandemic is having on Americans.
  Though public health experts have long noted that lockdowns are indeed draconian, they also acknowledge that they’ve been necessary to curb the insidious spread of the novel coronavirus, SARS-CoV-2. Such movement restrictions and distancing measures have largely been effective at controlling outbreaks in countries worldwide—apart from in the United States, of course, which has failed spectacularly at managing the deadly pandemic.
 Still, Caputo and McCance-Katz dismissed the mitigation strategy as one promoted only by rich people, who can easily manage to stay at home. “The people who say ‘It’s safer at home. Stay at home,’ tend to be people who are fairly affluent,” Caputo said.
 “Yeah, it probably is safer at home for them,” McCance-Katz responded. “They go to some nice house, some big house with all the amenities.”
 They went on to suggest that activities such as going to a football game or a movie theater are mainly enjoyed by people of the “lower strata of our economic system" and that is why those activities have been restricted. Meanwhile, “wealthy people with the house on the beach, they’re watching Netflix and every other streaming platform,” Caputo added. People who are essential workers, such as those who work in hospitals, “don’t have access to these subscriptions,” he said.
 “And I’ll just, I’m going to say it,” McCance-Katz said soon after. “We shut down the entire country before the virus, in my opinion, had a chance to get around the entire country. Why?” The stay-at-home orders are akin to using “a sledge hammer when I think we needed a scalpel,” she added.
 Caputo agreed, saying, “No doubt. And you know what? To me, the damage is done.”
  The two go on to discuss the pandemic’s impact on children, expressing disgust that in-person learning has been suspended in many places to try to curb disease spread. “What is this nonsense that somehow it’s unsafe to return to school?” McCance-Katz asked after noting that most children infected with SARS-CoV-2 do not get seriously ill. Though moments before they noted that people in the “lower strata” often live in more crowded, multigenerational homes, they failed to discuss the risk of children passing on the virus to family members and other people in their communities.
 Caputo—who, as noted, has been interfering with CDC’s scientific reports on the pandemic—moved on to blaming the US media for being “dishonest” and warping the public’s perception of the virus. He also blamed the media for “knocking the president” and “trampling” optimism over COVID-19 treatments and vaccines. “I don't think the United States media gives a damn about public health information,” he said.
  Toward the end, McCance-Katz offered a lighter outlook. Though she speculated that “thousands” might die from pandemic-related despair, she argued that Americans are resilient and that “the despair will abate.”
 Caputo, however, ended on a darker, confusing note. Though he had spent much of the episode downplaying the pandemic, he concluded by emphasizing the seriousness of COVID-19 and its death toll. “I find myself every morning, the first time I use my voice, I’m talking about death,” he said.
 “We hear reports in our meetings... These people [with COVID-19], they drown in their own fluids,” he said. “And the doctors have told me that it is the most profound fear that one can have. And that the looks on their faces when they’re dying is just something that they can't forget. These doctors can’t forget it. And the way—and I talk—this is the first thing I talk about in the morning.”
 According to a fresh report by Politico, Caputo called an emergency staff meeting Tuesday to apologize to HHS employees about his comments on the Facebook live video, in which he claimed the CDC was harboring a “resistance unit.” Some HHS staff told Politico they got the impression Caputo planned to step down.
 To date, the United States has reported more than 6.5 million cases of COVID-19 and over 195,000 deaths.
title: 疯狂的联邦播客轻描淡写新冠肺炎，抨击健康限制
title_en: Bonkers federal podcast downplays COVID-19, blasts health restrictions

note:
亚利桑那州大陪审团指控优步(Uber)自动驾驶汽车项目前安全司机拉斐拉·瓦斯克斯(Rafaela Vasquez)2018年在亚利桑那州坦佩(Tempe)杀害行人伊莱恩·赫兹伯格(Elaine Herzberg)。检察官去年决定不对优步提起刑事指控。
撞车事故发生在天黑后，在米尔大道一段灯光充足的地方。赫兹伯格骑着自行车穿过多车道公路时，优步SUV以每小时38英里的速度撞上了她。面对司机的摄像头拍摄的画面显示，就在撞车前，瓦斯克斯低头看了她的膝盖超过5秒钟。官员们发现，就在坠机前，瓦斯克斯一直在向她的手机播放名为The Voice的真人秀节目。
瓦斯克斯被控过失杀人罪，这一指控类似于过失杀人罪，建议判处2.5年监禁。然而，大陪审团也指控这起犯罪是用一种危险的工具--即汽车--犯下的。如果使用危险武器或工具实施过失杀人罪，建议的刑期增加到6年。
马里科帕县的一名律师在一份新闻稿中说，分心驾驶在我们社区是一个非常重要的问题。当司机驾驶汽车时，他们有责任以安全和守法的方式控制和操作车辆。
大陪审团在8月份正式批准了这些指控，瓦斯克斯于周二被传讯。她被释放等待审判，必须佩戴脚踝监测装置。
在赫兹伯格撞车事件发生前几个月，优步高级经理就收到了关于自动驾驶技术问题的警告。事实上，即将离任的优步工程师罗比·米勒(Robbie Miller)在赫兹伯格去世前几天向自动驾驶项目负责人发送了一封措辞严厉的电子邮件。
米勒写道，2月份几乎每隔一天就有一辆车受损。我们不应该每隔15,000英里就撞到东西。
米勒提到了前一周(赫兹伯格去世前9天)发生的一起事件，当时一辆优步测试车在人行道上行驶了几米。
米勒写道，在Waymo，如果一辆车表现出同样的行为，整个车队立即停飞数周或更长时间，我不会感到惊讶。相反，他报告说，这一事件基本上被忽视了，直到米勒让管理层注意到这一点。
一名检察官写道，在对提交的所有证据进行了非常彻底的审查后，该办公室确定优步公司没有刑事责任的基础。
优步与赫兹伯格的继承人迅速达成和解。但这起撞车事故破坏了优步羽翼未丰的自动驾驶技术项目。优步将该项目关闭了几个月，并永久结束了在亚利桑那州的测试。事故发生九个月后，优步终于在匹兹堡恢复了规模小得多的测试。
我想我理解不起诉优步的决定。当司机基本上放弃了工作，通过看电视超出了她的雇佣范围时，你如何让陪审团相信优步是有过错的？故事中提到的所有其他情况都涉及危险情况，但优步可以指出员工在其雇佣范围内采取的行动，以防止对人员造成任何伤害。“陪审团的女士们先生们，我的当事人正在测试一种新的系统，并聘请了另一名被告来监督该系统。我客户的其他雇员已经操作了这个系统，这样人们就不会受伤。直到优步员工为了看电视而辞去工作，没有请求或寻求救济，我们才以今天带到法庭上的悲剧告终。“。该软件存在缺陷，我愿意认为民事损害赔偿和对他们自动驾驶声誉的破坏足以惩罚和补救他们的决策，但在不了解更多的情况下，我很难将他们的决定归咎于检察官。
note_en:
An Arizona grand jury has  indicted Rafaela Vasquez, a former safety driver in Uber's self-driving car project, for the 2018 death of pedestrian Elaine Herzberg in Tempe, Arizona. Prosecutors  decided not to charge Uber criminally last year.
 The crash occurred after dark on a  well-lit stretch of Mill Avenue. Herzberg was crossing the multilane road with her bicycle when the Uber SUV struck her at 38 miles per hour. Footage from a driver-facing camera shows Vasquez  looking down at her lap for more than five seconds just prior to the crash. Officials found that Vasquez had been  streaming a reality show called  The Voice to her phone just before the crash.
 Vasquez is being charged with negligent homicide, a charge similar to manslaughter that carries a recommended sentence of 2.5 years. However, the grand jury also charged that the crime was committed with a "dangerous instrument"—namely the car. When negligent homicide is committed with a dangerous weapon or instrument, the recommended sentence increases to six years.
 "Distracted driving is an issue of great importance in our community," a Maricopa County attorney  said in a press release. "When a driver gets behind the wheel of a car, they have a responsibility to control and operate that vehicle safely and in a law-abiding manner."
 The grand jury formally approved the charges in August, and Vasquez was arraigned on Tuesday. She was freed pending trial and must wear an ankle monitoring device.
  Senior Uber managers were warned about problems with the self-driving technology in the months before the Herzberg crash. Indeed, one departing Uber engineer, Robbie Miller, sent a  scathing email to the head of the self-driving program days before Herzberg's death.
 "A car was damaged nearly every other day in February," Miller wrote. "We shouldn't be hitting things every 15,000 miles."
 Miller pointed to an incident the previous week (nine days before Herzberg's death) when an Uber test vehicle "drove on the sidewalk for several meters."
 "At Waymo I would not have been surprised if the entire fleet was immediately grounded for weeks or longer if a vehicle exhibited the same behavior," Miller wrote. Instead, he reported, the incident "was essentially ignored" until Miller brought it to the attention of management.
  "After a very thorough review of all evidence presented, this office has determined that there is no basis for criminal liability for the Uber corporation," a prosecutor  wrote.
 Uber  struck a quick settlement with Herzberg's heirs. But the crash shattered Uber's fledgling self-driving technology program. Uber shut the program down for months and permanently ended testing in Arizona. Nine months after the crash, Uber finally  resumed testing on a much smaller scale in Pittsburgh.
  I think I understand the decision not to prosecute Uber here.   How do you convince a jury that Uber was at fault when the driver had essentially abandoned her work and was acting outside the scope of her employment by watching TV? All of the other instances referenced in the story involve dangerous situations, but Uber can point to the actions of employees who were acting within the scope of their employment to prevent any harm to persons.   “Ladies and gentlemen of the jury, my client was testing a new system and hired the other defendant to monitor that system. My client’s other employees have operated the system so that people don’t get hurt. It was only when Uber’s employee abandoned her job in order to watch television, without requesting or seeking relief, that we ended up with the tragedy that brings here in court today.”  The software was flawed and I like to think that civil damages and the destruction of their self-driving reputation were enough to both punish and remediate their decision-making, but without knowing more I have a hard time blaming the prosecutors for their decision.
title: 2018年优步撞车事故安全司机被控过失杀人罪
title_en: Safety driver in 2018 Uber crash is charged with negligent homicide

note:
自然界中有许多奇妙的地质构造，从爱尔兰的巨人堤道到犹他州的卡斯尔顿大厦，科学家们长期以来一直对这些结构的形成过程感兴趣。纽约大学的一个应用数学家团队将注意力转向了中国和马达加斯加某些地区常见的所谓石林。纽约大学的研究小组表示，这些尖尖的岩层，就像中国云南省著名的石林一样，是固体在重力作用下溶解成液体的结果，产生了自然对流。他们在最近发表在“美国国家科学院院刊”上的一篇论文中描述了他们的发现。
合著者Leif Ristroph告诉Ars，他在纽约大学应用数学实验室的团队开始通过某种间接的途径对石林(严格地说是一种喀斯特地形)的研究产生了兴趣。他们使用模拟和实验来探索景观中由于一些塑造过程而演变出的有趣的形状，其中最明显的是侵蚀和溶解。
他说：当我们把糖果放在水箱里时，我们第一次发现了溶解形成的尖刺，后来回来发现了一个针状的尖顶。这位研究生，第一作者Mac Huang，甚至在欣赏这个形状的时候不小心割伤了自己。这就把我们带入了这个问题，当我们意识到与石尖和石林的联系时，我们非常兴奋，它们在它们的发展过程中一直是相当神秘的。我们希望我们的实验能在这些地貌背后讲述一个简单的起源故事。
为了在实验室中测试他们的模拟，研究小组将颗粒状的食糖、玉米糖浆和模子中的水结合在一起，制成了凝固(硬裂纹)糖果的块状和单柱状-类似于通常形成喀斯特地貌的可溶性岩石。积木的模具包括直立的金属棒阵列，用来播种有气孔的积木，这样就更接近了。他们将这些糖块和柱子放在一个装满室温脱气水的有机玻璃罐中-深度足以使溶解的糖沉淀在底部，远离被测试的物体。他们以每隔一分钟拍摄一张数码照片的方式捕捉到了溶解过程。
你可以在下面观看一段延时的实验视频，在视频中，一块正在溶解的糖果变成了一排尖锐的钉子，就像一床指甲。这块积木从内部毛孔开始，完全浸入水中，在那里溶解，成为一个糖果森林，然后倒塌。
即使在静止的水中也会发生这种情况。里斯特罗夫说：“我们发现，溶解过程本身就产生了雕刻尖峰形状的水流。”基本上，这种矿物--或者在我们的实验中，棒棒糖用作模拟岩石--溶解了，周围的液体变得很重，然后由于重力而向下流动。因此，我们的机制不需要任何特殊的流动条件或其他外部或环境条件：配方只需要溶解在液体和重力中即可。
Ristroph等人。这表明类似的机制在石林的形成过程中起着作用，只是时间尺度要长得多。像石灰石、白云石和石膏这样的可溶性岩石被淹没在水下，矿物质在那里慢慢溶解到周围的水中。较重的水在重力的向下拉力下沉，水流逐渐形成岩溶地貌。当洪水退去时，柱子和石林就会出现。
从表面上看，这些石林看起来非常类似于安第斯冰川高处发现的在非常干燥的空气中形成的雪柱。一些物理学家已经提出，当阳光将雪直接蒸发成水蒸气，而不是通过水相(升华)时，就形成了忏悔柱。微小的波峰和波谷形成，阳光被困在其中，产生额外的热量，形成更深的波谷，这些曲面反过来充当透镜，进一步加快了升华过程。另一种方案增加了一种额外的机制来解释柱状物奇怪的周期性固定间距：蒸汽扩散和热传输的组合产生了陡峭的温度梯度，从而产生了更高的升华速率。
物理学家已经能够在实验室中重现人造版的忏悔者。但就形成机制而言，忏悔者和石林实际上是截然不同的。里斯特罗夫说：我认为它们的相似之处相当肤浅。当然，雕刻过程在主要的驱动效果方面是非常不同的。主要是，我们的尖峰在很大程度上是由流动雕刻而成的，我认为这在忏悔者的形成中不会起到很大的作用。
诚然，纽约大学的研究人员在理想化的条件下获得了他们的结果-根据作者的说法，故意这样做是为了更好地清楚地识别和描述磨砺过程、潜在机制和数学结构。因此，作者写道，这项研究揭示了针头和指甲床图案所必需的最低限度的成分。未来，他们希望在实验室的不同环境条件下进一步测试这一形成过程，比如降水和地表径流，或者被埋在松散的沉积物下，可能会影响顶峰的形成。
title: 数学家可能已经揭开了“石林”形成的秘密。
title_en: Mathematicians may have unlocked the secret of how “stone forests” form

note:
上世纪90年代初，“超级马里奥全明星”(Super Mario All-Stars)是首批建议重要的老游戏机游戏--比如早期的2D超级马里奥兄弟系列--不应该搁置在最初托管它们的过时硬件上的游戏之一。这些收藏还普及了这样一种观念，即旧游戏可以用新技术改进，同时仍然保持它们的原始意图。
27年后的今天，广泛的向后兼容性和定期的Remaster版本(包括任天堂的版本)使这个概念更像是礼仪性的，而不是革命性的。因此，超级马里奥3D全明星让人感到奇怪的反高潮。
一方面，它汇集了有史以来最好的三个3D平台(至少是最好的两个半)，其格式更容易与现代电视和Switch方便的便携外形相兼容。这三个3D平台是有史以来最好的三个平台的集合(好吧，至少是最好的两个半)，其格式更容易与现代电视和Switch方便的便携外形兼容。另一方面，这几乎就是全部。
其他经典的游戏收藏试图用新的游戏功能和数字保存的历史文物来证明它们的价值。超级马里奥3D全明星是一个更基本的收藏，任天堂已经做了略高于让这些游戏在新硬件上运行所需的最低工作量。
(Ars Technica可能会通过会员计划从本帖子上的链接获得销售补偿。)。
需要明确的是，任天堂所做的不仅仅是将一些旧的ROM放入一个新的基于交换机的仿真器中。例如，每个游戏中的用户界面和游戏中的指令已经被更新，以具体涉及开关控制。(例如，“超级马里奥64”的简介屏幕上显示的是按下“开始”键，而不是按下“开始”键。)还有一个暂停菜单，可以让您在包含的三款游戏之间快速切换或阅读马里奥控件的单页参考。
3D全明星还更新了较旧的任天堂控制方案，以便与Switch Joy-Cons和Pro Controller配合使用。例如，在超级马里奥64中，原本映射到N64上的四个C按钮的摄像头移动现在通过轻弹右模拟杆来控制。这感觉有点奇怪，特别是在更灵活、可自由旋转的摄像头会受到欢迎的情况下(就像我们最近在未经授权的PC端口中看到的那样)。一般来说，虽然游戏的默认相机编程仍然提供了一个有用的和电影的视角，没有太多的玩家干扰。
超级马里奥银河需要更多的技巧来复制原始Wii遥控器的功能。其中之一，马里奥的所有重要的旋转冲压移动现在可以通过轻触Y按钮执行，除了正常的控制器摇动。这是一个受欢迎的变化，既提高了游戏中关键动作的精确度，又消除了在手持玩游戏期间不断摇动整个开关的需要。
不过，如果没有Wii遥控器，移动游戏屏幕上的明星光标(用来抓取和拍摄明星片段以及抓取某些游戏中的物品)就会变得更加棘手。在停靠模式下，光标在控制器的倾斜敏感陀螺仪检测到的非常微小的移动的基础上四处飞来飞去。这并不像Wii遥控器的红外线指针那样精确或直观，但经过一点练习后，它通常已经足够准确了。
不过，在便携模式下，您可以通过在屏幕上轻触或跟踪手指来控制星形光标。这是一个相当尴尬的要求，需要你暂时将一只手从开关的物理控制上暂时移走，同时用另一只手支撑开关的整个不平衡重量。对于一个困难的用例来说，这可能是最好的解决方案，但最主要的是它让我们想起了3DS触摸屏点击时最不舒服的一些时刻。
除了这些细微的变化，这些游戏的玩法和外观几乎与它们最初的同类游戏一模一样(在Super Mario 64和Sunsun上的帧率可以逆行到30fps)。我之所以这么说，几乎是因为这些游戏在Switch更强大的硬件上采用了任天堂所说的更高的图片分辨率。
这一变化的影响在“超级马里奥64”上最为明显。在那里，原始的UI元素，如像素化的、块状的文本，已经被在高清显示器上看起来平滑的字母所取代。虽然游戏中的敌人和物品仍然以其原始的、滑稽的低多边形模型出现，但它们的锐利边缘现在给人的印象是迷人的复古。
“超级马里奥64”的不足之处在于它的环境纹理，这些纹理似乎没有经过重新绘制，在更高分辨率的屏幕上看起来更清晰。因此，在大屏幕电视上，草地或落基山脉看起来就像涂抹的泥泞的烂摊子，不那么讨人喜欢。
note_en:
In the early '90s,  Super Mario All-Stars was among the first titles to suggest that important old console games—like the early 2D  Super Mario Bros. series—shouldn't be stranded on the obsolete hardware that originally hosted them. The collection also popularized the notion that older games could be improved with new technology while still preserving their original intent.
 Now 27 years later,  widespread backward compatibility and  regular remasters (including  those from  Nintendo) have made that concept more de rigueur than revolutionary. Thus,  Super Mario 3D All-Stars feels weirdly anti-climactic.
 On the one hand, it's a collection of three of the best 3D platformers ever made (well, two-and-a-half of the best, at least) in a format that's more easily compatible with modern TVs and the Switch's convenient portable form factor. On the other hand, that's pretty much all it is.
 Other classic gaming collections try to prove their value with  new gameplay features and digitally preserved historical artifacts.  Super Mario 3D All-Stars is a much more bare-bones collection, where Nintendo has done just above the minimum amount of work necessary to get these games functional on new hardware.
  (Ars Technica may earn compensation for sales from links on this post through  affiliate programs.)
To be clear, Nintendo has done a  little more than throw some old ROMs into a new Switch-based emulator. The user interface and in-game instructions in each game, for instance, have been updated to refer specifically to the Switch controls. (For example, the intro screen to  Super Mario 64 says "Press +" rather than "Press Start.") There's also a pause menu that lets you switch quickly between the three included games or read through a single-page reference for Mario's controls.
 3D All-Stars also updates older Nintendo control schemes to work with the Switch Joy-Cons and Pro Controller. In  Super Mario 64, for instance, the camera movement that was mapped to the four C-buttons on the N64 is now controlled by flicking the right analog stick. That feels a little weird, especially in situations where a more flexible, freely rotatable camera would be welcome (as we've seen in  recent unauthorized PC ports). In general, though, the game's default camera programming still usually delivers a useful and cinematic viewing angle without much player interference.
    Super Mario Galaxy needs a bit more finessing to replicate the functions of the original Wii Remote. For one, Mario's all-important spinning punch move can now be performed with a tap of the Y button, in addition to the normal controller shake. This is a welcome change that both increases the precision of a crucial in-game action and eliminates the need to constantly shake the entire Switch during handheld play.
 Without a Wii Remote, though, it's trickier to move the game's on-screen "star" cursor (used to pick up and shoot star bits and grab certain in-game objects). In docked mode, the cursor flits around based on very small movements detected by the controller's tilt-sensitive gyroscope. This isn't as precise or intuitive as the Wii Remote's IR pointer, but after a little practice, it's generally accurate enough.
 In portable mode, though, you control the star cursor by tapping or tracing your finger across the screen. This is a pretty awkward ask, requiring you to temporarily remove one hand from the Switch's physical controls briefly while supporting the entire unbalanced weight of the Switch with the other. It may well be the best solution possible for a difficult use case, but mostly it reminds us of  some of the most uncomfortable moments of 3DS touchscreen tapping.
  Aside from those minor changes, these games play and look almost exactly like their original counterparts (right down to retrograde 30fps frame rates on  Super Mario 64 and  Sunshine). I say "almost" because the games feature what Nintendo describes as "improved picture resolution" on the Switch's more powerful hardware.
 The effects of this change are most noticeable on  Super Mario 64. There, original UI elements like pixellated, blocky text have been replaced with lettering that looks smooth on an HD display. And while in-game enemies and items still appear in their original, comically low-polygon models, their sharp edges now come across as charmingly retro.
 Where  Super Mario 64 suffers is in its environmental textures, which don't appear to have been redrawn to look any sharper on higher-resolution screens. Thus, fields of grass or rocky mountains look like smeared, muddy messes on a big-screen TV in a less-than-endearing way.
title: 超级马里奥3D全明星回顾，骨子里的怀旧扭曲地带
title_en: Super Mario 3D All-Stars review:  A bare-bones nostalgia warp zone

note:
在2020年，读到美国环境保护局(Environmental Protection Agency，简称EPA)取消污染法律或主张应该允许大企业为所欲为的报道似乎更常见。但显然，该机构偶尔也会按预期工作。本周早些时候，该公司与美国司法部(US Department Of Justice)和加州空气资源委员会(California Air Resources Board)一起，要求梅赛德斯-奔驰(Mercedes-Benz)的母公司戴姆勒股份公司(Daimler AG)对销售装有排放降低装置的柴油车负责。
EPA和CARB发现，在2015年大众汽车(Volkswagen)尾气排放丑闻之后，戴姆勒(Daimler)的柴油发动机出现了问题。美国环保署告诉戴姆勒，为了调查潜在的减效装置，它将对该公司的四缸和六缸柴油发动机进行一些额外的测试，使用正常操作和使用中可能合理预期的行驶周期和条件。
在这样做的过程中，它发现了几个辅助排放控制装置，这些装置在戴姆勒提交的同质文件中没有描述。从2009年到2016年，总共约有16万辆斯普林特面包车和约9万辆梅赛德斯-奔驰汽车受到影响。
因此，戴姆勒将支付8.75亿美元的民事罚款，以及7030万美元的其他罚款。它还必须支付召回费用，以修复这25万台柴油发动机。所有这些都需要新的氮氧化物(NOx)过滤器和软件更新，但许多还需要额外的部件，包括新的铜催化剂、柴油颗粒过滤器、传感器，甚至新的仪表板。
这将使该公司额外损失4.36亿美元，而且它也不能再等下去了。和解协议要求85%的梅赛德斯-奔驰乘用车必须在两年内召回和维修，85%的斯普林特面包车必须在三年内维修，失败的将受到严厉处罚。
在公司支票簿出炉期间，戴姆勒还将向加州支付1.1亿美元，为该州的污染缓解项目提供资金，并已被勒令购买15台新的机车发动机，以更换一些更旧、更脏的发动机。除了这些经济处罚之外，戴姆勒的员工未来还将接受更多的合规培训，这是旨在防止此类事情再次发生的公司改革的一部分。
note_en:
In 2020 it seems more usual  to read about the US Environmental Protection Agency  rolling back pollution laws or  arguing that big business should be  allowed to do what it wants. But apparently the agency does occasionally work as intended. Earlier this week, together with the US Department of Justice and the California Air Resources Board, it held Daimler AG—parent company to Mercedes-Benz—accountable for selling diesel vehicles fitted with emissions defeat devices.
 EPA and CARB found that all was not right with the Daimler's diesel engines in the wake of the  2015 Volkswagen emissions scandal. EPA told Daimler it was going to conduct some additional tests of the company's four- and six-cylinder diesel engines "using driving cycles and conditions that may reasonably be expected to be encountered in normal operation and use, for the purposes of investigating a potential defeat device."
 In doing so, it discovered several auxiliary emission control devices that were not described in the  homologation paperwork submitted by Daimler. In total,  about 160,000 Sprinter vans and about 90,000 Mercedes-Benz vehicles are affected, between model years 2009 and 2016.
  As a consequence, Daimler will pay $875 million in civil penalties, and $70.3 million in other penalties. It will also have to pay for a recall to fix those 250,000 Diesel engines. All of them will require a new Nitrogen Oxide (NOx) filter and a software update, but many will also require additional parts, including new copper catalysts, diesel particulate filters, sensors, and even new instrument panels.
 That's going to cost the company an additional $436 million, and it can't hang around, either. The settlement requires that 85 percent of Mercedes-Benz passenger cars have to be recalled and fixed within two years, and 85 percent of the Sprinter vans must be repaired within three, with "stiff penalties" for failing.
 While the company checkbook is out, Daimler will also pay California $110 million to fund pollution-mitigation projects within the state, and it has been ordered to buy 15 new locomotive engines to replace some older, dirtier ones. On top of those financial penalties, Daimler employees are in for a whole lot more compliance training in their future as part of corporate reforms intended to prevent this kind of thing from happening again.
title: 戴姆勒肮脏的柴油减效装置交易：15亿美元道歉
title_en: Daimler’s dirty diesel defeat device deal: $1.5 billion to say sorry

note:
波音公司(Boeing)向飞行员和监管机构隐瞒了其737 Max喷气式飞机的设计缺陷，因为波音公司正在竞相将飞机认证为适合飞行，这份谴责国会的报告显示，去年两架飞机相继坠毁，造成346人死亡。
美国众议院运输委员会的这份报告发现，这家美国飞机制造商偷工减料，并向监管机构施压，要求其在试图追赶欧洲竞争对手空客(Airbus)的过程中忽略其新设计的各个方面。它还指责美国监管机构过于关注取悦该公司，无法进行适当的监督。
报告称：“(这两起坠机事件)是波音工程师一系列错误的技术假设、波音管理层缺乏透明度以及(联邦航空管理局)监管严重不足的可怕结果--联邦航空局在履行严格监督波音和确保飞行公众安全的职责方面被监管俘虏，这是有害的结果。”他说，“(这两起坠机事件)是波音工程师一系列错误的技术假设、波音管理层缺乏透明度以及联邦航空局(Federal Aviation Administration)监管严重不足的结果。
“这份报告中列出的事实证明了波音公司令人不安的技术误判和令人不安的管理误判的模式。它还暴露了联邦航空局的许多监督失误和问责漏洞，这些漏洞在波音737 MAX坠机事件中发挥了重要作用。“。
自去年以来，波音公司一直在接受多项调查，当时埃塞俄比亚航空公司运营的一架MAX喷气式飞机坠毁，就在五个月前，印尼狮子航空公司(Lion Air)拥有的另一架飞机坠入海中。
调查人员发现，在这两种情况下，有故障的传感器都导致自动反失速系统错误启动，迫使飞机机头向下。狮子航空公司和埃塞俄比亚航空公司的飞行员都在努力纠正他们的喷气式飞机，但每次这样做都被自动系统超越。
自去年4月以来，国会议员一直在对这些事故进行自己的调查。周三的报告标志着长达17个月的调查进入高潮，调查涉及5次公开听证会，24次采访，以及60万页的文件。
这份长达238页的报告详细介绍了波音公司是如何试图将驾驶新MAX所需的监管测试和飞行员培训降至最低的。为了与空客A320neo竞争，新MAX正在仓促推出。
调查发现，该公司成功地说服了美国联邦航空局(FAA)不要将反失速系统归类为“安全关键”，这意味着许多飞行员甚至在驾驶MAX之前都不知道它的存在。
在这样做的过程中，波音向监管机构隐瞒了内部测试数据，这些数据显示，如果飞行员花了超过10秒的时间才意识到系统错误地启动，后果将是“灾难性的”。
报告还详细说明了警报是如何在MAX机队的绝大多数机队上不起作用的，该警报本应警告飞行员他们的一个反失速传感器存在潜在问题。它发现，该公司在继续在世界各地推出新飞机时，故意向飞行员和监管机构隐瞒了这一事实。
一年多来，波音公司一直在努力纠正MAX中发现的故障，最近表示，希望在第三季度再次开始交付这款喷气式飞机。
该公司表示：“MAX的修订设计已经接受了密集的内部和监管审查，包括超过37.5万个工程和测试小时，以及1300次试飞。一旦联邦航空局和其他监管机构确定MAX可以安全恢复服务，它将是历史上审查最彻底的飞机之一。“。
虽然该公司在试图认证MAX时走捷径，但委员会在联邦航空局发现了一个合规的监管机构。
美国联邦航空局通过在很大程度上依赖“授权代表”来认证新的飞机设计，授权代表是公司员工，他们被监管机构授权验证某些设计和系统。报告发现，波音有几次没有向监管机构标记重要信息。
国会议员已经提出立法，将加强联邦航空局的飞机认证程序，包括对公司雇用的代表进行定期独立审计。
联邦航空局在一份声明中表示：“联邦航空局致力于不断推进航空安全，并期待与委员会合作，实施其报告中确定的改进措施。”

note_en:
Boeing hid design flaws in its 737 Max jet from both pilots and regulators as it raced to have the airplane certified as fit to fly, according to a damning congressional report into why two of the aircraft crashed within months of each other last year, killing 346 people.
 The report by the US House of Representatives transport committee found the US aircraft maker cut corners and pressured regulators to overlook aspects of its new design in its attempts to catch up with European rival Airbus. It also accused US regulators of being too concerned with pleasing the company to exercise proper oversight.
 The report said: “[The two crashes] were the horrific culmination of a series of faulty technical assumptions by Boeing’s engineers, a lack of transparency on the part of Boeing’s management, and grossly insufficient oversight by the [Federal Aviation Administration]—the pernicious result of regulatory capture on the part of the FAA with respect to its responsibilities to perform robust oversight of Boeing and to ensure the safety of the flying public.
 “The facts laid out in this report document a disturbing pattern of technical miscalculations and troubling management misjudgments made by Boeing. It also illuminates numerous oversight lapses and accountability gaps by the FAA that played a significant role in the 737 Max crashes.”
 Boeing has been under multiple investigations since last year, when a Max jet operated by Ethiopian Airlines crashed just five months after another owned by Indonesia’s Lion Air plunged into the sea.
 Investigators have found that on both occasions, a faulty sensor caused an automatic anti-stall system to kick in erroneously, forcing the airplane's nose downwards. Pilots for both Lion Air and Ethiopian Airlines battled to right their jets but were overridden by the automatic system each time they did so.
 Members of Congress have been conducting their own probe into the accidents since last April. Wednesday’s report marks the culmination of 17 months of investigation, involving five public hearings, 24 interviews, and 600,000 pages of documents.
 The 238-page report details how Boeing attempted to minimize both the regulatory testing and pilot training required to fly the new Max, which was being rushed out in an attempt to compete with the Airbus A320neo.
 It found the company successfully persuaded the FAA not to classify the anti-stall system as “safety critical,” meaning that many pilots did not even know of its existence before flying the Max.
 In doing so, Boeing concealed from regulators internal test data showing that if a pilot took longer than 10 seconds to recognize that the system had kicked in erroneously, the consequences would be “catastrophic.”
 The report also detailed how an alert, which would have warned pilots of a potential problem with one of their anti-stall sensors, was not working on the vast majority of the Max fleet. It found that the company deliberately concealed this fact from both pilots and regulators as it continued to roll out the new aircraft around the world.
 Boeing has been working to correct the faults found in the Max for more than a year and recently said it hoped to begin delivering the jet again in the third quarter.
 The company said: “The revised design of the Max has received intensive internal and regulatory review, including more than 375,000 engineering and test hours and 1,300 test flights. Once the FAA and other regulators have determined the Max can safely return to service, it will be one of the most thoroughly scrutinized aircraft in history.”
 While the company cut corners in its attempts to certify the Max, the committee found a compliant regulator in the FAA.
 The FAA certifies new aircraft designs by relying heavily on “authorized representatives,” company employees who are authorized by the regulator to validate certain designs and systems. And the report found that on several occasions Boeing did not flag important pieces of information to the regulator.
 Members of Congress have introduced legislation that would toughen the FAA’s aircraft certification process, including carrying out regular independent audits on company-employed representatives.
 The FAA said in a statement: “The FAA is committed to continually advancing aviation safety and looks forward to working with the committee to implement improvements identified in its report.”

title: 波音向飞行员和监管机构隐瞒737 MAX喷气式飞机的设计缺陷
title_en: Boeing hid design flaws in 737 Max jets from pilots and regulators

note:
Facebook本周实施了另一项新举措，旨在打击该平台上猖獗、危险的虚假信息-这一次，与气候危机有关。不幸的是，就像之前的无数其他举措一样，这一举措似乎可能会在大约一周的时间里成为正面的头条新闻，然后默默无闻地消失，在此过程中解决Facebook的更深层次问题的确切程度为零。
脸书在一篇公司博客文章中写道，我们从新冠肺炎事件中学到的最大教训之一是，在全球危机期间，脸书在将人们与准确的、专家的建议和信息联系起来方面可以发挥多么强大的作用。为此，Facebook正在推出一个新的气候变化信息中心(Climate Change Information Center)模块和登录页面，从理论上讲，它将把用户连接到最新的、基于事实的、现实基础上的信息。
美国和少数其他国家的用户可能已经在本周的新闻馈送中看到了关于新气候信息中心的通知，无论是在桌面上还是在移动设备上。绿色的盒子恳请你点名去看看我们的气候是如何变化的。
点击直接进入气候科学信息中心，内容包括一些气候事实、一些经过审查的组织的链接、根据您所在位置定制的温度事实图表，以及从主要媒体(如今日美国报、VOX、纽约时报、BuzzFeed和CNN)精心挑选的相关新闻报道。用户还可以选择关注或喜欢平台上的任何其他页面、组或个人。
Facebook表示，它以新冠肺炎信息登录页面为模型建立了气候信息中心，在冠状病毒错误信息和虚假信息已经在社交媒体平台上广泛传播后，该页面于今年春天推出。该公司表示，约有6亿用户在某一时刻点击查看了新冠肺炎的信息模块-约占脸书27亿月度活跃用户的22%。
谎言、虚假和虚假信息对Facebook来说并不是一个新问题-不幸的是，该公司在处理所有类型的错误信息方面的记录参差不齐。
该公司首席执行官马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)长期以来一直坚称，他对抗Facebook上不良信息的首选解决方案是在Facebook上发布更多内容。Facebook并没有禁止大多数形式的虚假作为一项政策，而是充其量给它们贴上了一个事实核查的链接，并鼓励用户发布自己的、理想情况下更准确的信息来反驳虚假。
当然，这一理论认为，有些人(包括现任政客)可能会利用Facebook做怪人，但其他每个人都有责任利用Facebook说出真实的事情，并希望真相能抵消谎言。
然而，在这一点上，似乎很明显，政策并没有像扎克伯格希望的那样奏效。事实证明，散布谎言的人往往不是在诚信经营，现在Facebook面临着来自各方的更多批评。
Facebook上虚假信息的传播可能会对现实世界产生可怕的影响。到目前为止，俄罗斯利用社交媒体操纵2016年美国总统大选的影响是众所周知的。2018年，该公司承认，缅甸军方发布的虚假内容直接助长了该国罗辛亚少数民族的种族灭绝。就在本周，Facebook的一名前数据科学家写了一份谴责备忘录，描述了她在Facebook工作的两年里，在Facebook上的几次不真实的政治行为在全球范围内煽风点火。
不真实的演员也不是Facebook面临的唯一问题。很多散布虚假信息的人和组织都非常清楚他们是谁，他们在做什么。例如，反疫苗接种团体将错误信息传播得很远很广。Facebook禁止反VAX团体在该平台上发布广告，但有机内容仍能产生参与度，尽管Facebook贴上了事实核查标签。现在，Facebook甚至发现自己面临着来自反华而不实者的诉讼，这些人不喜欢被事实核查。
脸书对重大话题的事实核查标签，如选举安全、新冠肺炎，以及现在的气候变化，都通向他们的信息中心。事实上，以主题为中心的登录页面确实将用户引导到关于手头主题的高质量、可靠、本地和全国性的信息，正如他们所设计的那样。
然而，这些中心很快就会被淹没在其他Facebook内容的海洋中，你可能会使用，也可能不会使用，或者实际上，你会意识到存在。举个例子，这张Facebook手机菜单的图片是在9月15日拼凑而成的：
距离大选还有不到50天的时间--我所在的州将于本周五开始提前投票--很高兴看到Facebook自吹自擂的选举信息中心一直排在榜单的末尾，排在我几乎从未使用过的其他近20多项功能的后面。
气候变化信息中心正面临着另一个完全由Facebook自己制造的严峻挑战：该公司显然不愿激怒那些分享关于气候变化的虚假信息的有权势的、受欢迎的团体和个人。
Facebook在气候变化问题上的立场是，大多数否认都是见仁见智的问题，因此不受事实核查的约束。
Facebook公关总监安迪·斯通(Andy Stone)在7月份告诉“纽约时报”(New York Times)，当有人发布基于虚假事实的内容时--即使是一篇评论或社论--它仍然有资格接受事实核查。我们正在努力在我们的指导方针中更清楚地说明这一点，这样我们的事实核查人员就可以利用他们的判断来确定这是否是试图以意见为幌子掩盖虚假信息。
然而，Facebook至少两次推翻了事实核查专家的裁决，这些专家确实审查了与气候相关的帖子，发现它们部分或全部是错误的。气候反馈是一个与Facebook合作作为其事实核查机构之一的组织，该组织将2019年华盛顿考官的一篇专栏文章标记为虚假。否认气候变化的组织二氧化碳联盟(CO2 Coalition)向Facebook抱怨事实核查，Facebook去掉了帖子上的标签。
今年早些时候，Facebook最大的出版商、保守派网站“每日电讯报”(Daily Wire)发表的一篇关于气候变化的文章，获得了“气候反馈”(Climate Feedback)部分失实的评级。时事通讯“大众信息”(Popular Information)获得的Facebook内部文件显示，Facebook员工同意部分错误的评级，但在“每日电讯报”(Daily Wire)文章的作者抱怨被审查后，Facebook还是撤回了这个标签。
简而言之：事实核查是不够的--特别是如果Facebook在有人投诉时就会退缩的话。
一个由媒体和环境监督团体组成的联盟-包括塞拉俱乐部、绿色和平和忧思科学家联盟-发表了一份联合声明，将他们的行为一半归功于Facebook。
这些组织写道，这项新政策是向前迈出的一小步，但并没有解决隐藏在人们眼前的更大的气候虚假信息危机。否认气候变化的人是一个很容易定义的群体-我们给了Facebook这个名单。正如我们在Facebook后院的火灾、活跃的飓风季节和极端天气中看到的那样，气候变化的危险是紧迫的、真实的和致命的。就像Facebook为自己的碳排放承担责任一样，它也必须承担责任，阻止否认气候的人在其平台上散布虚假信息。
note_en:
Facebook this week implemented yet another new initiative meant to combat rampant, dangerous disinformation on the platform—this time, relating to the climate crisis. Unfortunately this initiative, like countless others before it, seems likely to generate positive headlines for about a week before disappearing unremarked into obscurity, solving exactly zero of Facebook's deeper problems along the way.
 "One of the biggest lessons we have learned from the COVID-19 pandemic is how powerful Facebook can be for connecting people to accurate, expert advice and information during a global crisis," Facebook wrote in a  corporate blog post. To that end, Facebook is launching a new "Climate Change Information Center" module and landing page that will, in theory, connect users to up-to-date, fact-based information grounded in reality.
 Users in the US and a small handful of other countries may already have seen a notification about the new climate information center appear in their newsfeeds this week, either on desktop or on mobile. The green box implores you by name to "see how our climate is changing."
 Clicking through takes you directly to the  climate science information center, featuring a handful of climate facts, links to a selection of vetted organizations, graphs of temperature facts customized to your location, and a curated selection of relevant news stories from major outlets such as USA Today, Vox, The New York Times, BuzzFeed, and CNN. Users can also choose to follow or like any other page, group, or individual on the platform.
 Facebook said it modeled the climate information center on its  COVID-19 information landing page, which it launched this spring  after coronavirus misinformation and falsehoods were already widespread across social media platforms. About 600 million users have at one point or another clicked through to view the COVID-19 information module, the company said—about 22 percent of Facebook's 2.7 billion monthly active users.
  Lies, falsehoods, and disinformation are not a new problem for Facebook—and the company unfortunately has a spotty track record when it comes to handling misinformation of all types.
 Company CEO Mark Zuckerberg has long been  adamant that his preferred solution for countering bad stuff on Facebook is to have  more stuff on Facebook. Rather than banning most forms of falsehood as a policy, Facebook at best labels them with a link to a fact check and encourages users to post their own, ideally more accurate information to counter untruths.
 Sure, the theory goes, some people (including  sitting politicians) may use Facebook to be cranks, but it's  incumbent on everyone else to use Facebook to say things that are true and hope that the truths can counteract the lies.
 At this point, however, it seems clear that policy is not working out as well as Zuckerberg may have hoped. People who spread falsehoods, it turns out, tend not to be operating in good faith, and now Facebook faces even more criticism from all sides.
 The spread of false information on Facebook can have dire real-world effects. The impact of  Russia's use of social media to manipulate the 2016 US presidential election is by now  well known. In 2018, the company  admitted that false content  posted by Myanmar's military was directly contributing to the  genocide of the Rohingya ethnic minority in that nation. As recently as this week, a former data scientist for Facebook  wrote a damning memo describing several instances of inauthentic political behavior on Facebook fanning flames worldwide during her two years with the company.
 Nor are inauthentic actors the only problem Facebook faces. Plenty of people and organizations who spread false information are extremely clear about who they are and what they're doing. Anti-vaccination groups, for example, spread misinformation far and wide. Facebook prohibits anti-vax groups from posting advertisements on the platform, but organic content still generates engagement, even though Facebook applies fact-checking labels. Now, Facebook even finds itself  facing a lawsuit from anti-vaxxers who don't like being fact-checked.
  Facebook's fact-check labels for major topics such as election security, COVID-19, and now climate change lead to their information centers. The topic-centered landing pages do in fact direct users to good-quality, reliable, local, and national information on the topics at hand, as they are designed to.
 These centers, however, rapidly end up buried in a sea of other Facebook content you may or may not ever use or, in fact, realize exists. This image of Facebook's mobile menu, for example, was assembled on September 15:
  With the election coming up in fewer than 50 days—and early voting beginning in my state this Friday—it's good to see that Facebook's vaunted election information hub is all the way down at the bottom of the list, beneath nearly two dozen other features I have literally never used.
  The climate change information center is facing another steep challenge entirely of Facebook's own making: the company's apparent unwillingness to anger powerful, popular groups and individuals that share false information about climate change.
 Facebook's stance on climate change is that most denial is  a matter of opinion and therefore not subject to fact-checking.
 "When someone posts content based on false facts—even if it’s an op-ed or editorial—it is still eligible for fact-checking," Facebook Communications Director Andy Stone told The New York Times  in July. "We're working to make this clearer in our guidelines so our fact checkers can use their judgment to determine whether it is an attempt to mask false information under the guise of opinion."
 However, Facebook has at least twice overturned the rulings of fact-checking experts who  did review climate-related posts and found them to be partly or entirely false. Climate Feedback, a group that partners with Facebook as one of its fact-checkers, marked a 2019 Washington Examiner op-ed as false. A climate-change-denial organization, the CO2 Coalition,  complained to Facebook about the fact-check, and Facebook removed the label on the post.
 Earlier this year, an article about climate change published by The Daily Wire, a conservative site that is also  Facebook's biggest publisher, received a "partly false" rating from Climate Feedback. The newsletter  Popular Information obtained internal Facebook documents showing that Facebook staff agreed with the "partly false" rating, but Facebook withdrew the label anyway after the author of the Daily Wire article complained about being "censored."
 In short: fact-checking is not enough—especially if Facebook is just going to walk it back whenever someone complains.
 A coalition of media and environment watchdog groups—including the Sierra Club, Greenpeace, and the Union of Concerned Scientists—issued a  joint statement giving Facebook half credit for their actions.
 "This new policy is a small step forward but does not address the larger climate disinformation crisis hiding in plain sight," the groups wrote. "Climate deniers are an easy group to define—we gave Facebook the list. As we’ve seen with the fires in Facebook’s backyard, active hurricane season, and extreme weather, the dangers of climate change are urgent, real, and deadly. Just as Facebook has taken responsibility for its own carbon emissions, it must take responsibility to stop climate deniers from spreading disinformation on its platform."
title: Facebook为对抗气候变化否认所做的最新空洞努力
title_en: Facebook’s latest hollow effort to fight climate change denial

针对彭博社(Bloomberg)最近的一篇报道称，由于零部件短缺，索尼已经削减了即将推出的PlayStation5的产量，索尼罕见地发表了否认声明。
该公司在提供给GamesIndustry.biz的一份声明中表示：“虽然我们没有公布与制造相关的细节，但彭博社提供的信息是虚假的。”&#34；自从开始批量生产以来，我们没有更改过PlayStation5的生产编号。&#34；
彭博社(Bloomberg)的报告援引知情人士的话暗示，在截至2021年3月的财年，该公司将把全球游戏机的预期产量从1500万台降至1100万台。根据这份报告，这是由于其SOC的产量收益率低至50%。
索尼表示PS5的生产目标没有改变，这也与彭博社之前关于索尼据称尚未解决的PS5生产计划的报道相反。7月份，彭博社(Bloomberg)报道称，索尼已决定今年将其计划的制造能力大约翻一番，从500万或600万台增加到1000万台。
在此之前的4月份，彭博社(Bloomberg)报道称，索尼已决定生产比2013年PS4发布时少得多的PS5，部分原因是索尼预计PS5雄心勃勃的规格会导致发布时的高价，从而拖累需求。
作为背景，截至2014年3月，索尼生产了750万台PS4，其中至少有600万台在此期间通过消费者销售。据彭博社报道，在上市窗口期内，1000万台的减产目标仍将是一个相对较大的增长。
游戏机早期购买者被压抑的需求通常意味着主要新游戏机的第一批发货几乎立即销售一空，这意味着你不能对新游戏机销售的头几天有太多解读。不过，随着假日销售季度过渡到新的一年，游戏机制造商必须仔细调整产能，以避免令人沮丧的长期短缺或代价高昂的供应过剩。
游戏机制造商还必须应对与COVID相关的经济冲击对新游戏机硬件需求的潜在影响。今年早些时候，任天堂Switch的门票销售一空，因为在与流行病相关的停摆期间，玩家蜂拥至该系统进行娱乐。但微软在3月份表示，由于一些国家继续面临不断上升的失业率，它正在仔细监测需求方面的情况。
与终生销售数字相比，发布窗口游戏机的销售数字通常只是杯水车薪，但它们可以提供一个很好的迹象，表明未来几年游戏机玩家可能会青睐哪个平台。微软表示，增加市场份额的旧思维不如我们正在获得新客户、他们是否在购买游戏、他们是否在从事这项服务那么重要。(微软认为，增加市场份额的旧思维不如我们赢得新客户、他们是否购买游戏、是否从事这项服务更重要。)
title: 索尼驳斥了有关它正在降低PS5发布可用性的报道
title_en: Sony pushes back on reports that it’s reducing PS5’s launch availability

我们用千瓦时来衡量电动汽车电池，所以如果你花钱给一辆电动汽车充电，你可能会认为是按千瓦时充电的。现在，如果你在23个州或哥伦比亚特区中的一个使用电气化美国充电站，你就可以做到这一点，因为充电公司已经推出了新的定价结构。对于其余27个要求客户按分钟付费的州，该公司简化了计划，并降低了价格。
当Etrify America在2019年开始推出价值20亿美元的充电网络的第一阶段时，它的支付结构很复杂。当你将一辆电动汽车插入快速充电器时，作为握手过程的一部分，汽车会告诉充电器它可以接受的最大功率水平(以千瓦为单位)。
“电气化美国”用它来确定你每天支付多少钱，有三个不同的频段(0-75kW，76-125kW和126-350kW)，每个频段的价格都比前一个更高。正如我发现的那样，即使你的车在充电过程中以低得多的速度吸收电力，支付更高的费用也是很有可能的-理论上的最大千瓦值是设定价格的标准。(实际上，像起亚这样的原始设备制造商已经与电气化美国签署了协议，这样车主就可以在几年内获得折扣，甚至免费充电。)。
每分钟电价是美国许多州依法要求的。但其中23个(和DC)稍微明智一些，只要求客户支付用电量。(电气化美国表示，这些州占使用其网络进行的收费的78%。)。一段时间以来，特斯拉已经向这些州的超级增压器用户收取千瓦时的费用，现在电气化美国也将这样做。这意味着你将支付相同的费用在50kW的雪佛兰博尔特和270kW的保时捷泰康一样的千瓦时。
电气化美国通行证+会员的电费是0.31美元/千瓦时；如果你不想支付4美元的月费成为通行证+会员，费用是0.43美元/千瓦时。这比特斯拉目前在同一州收取的0.28美元/千瓦时更贵，尽管它不再向新车主提供免费的超级充电。
在要求电气化美国按分钟计费的州，它将其结构简化为只有两个等级-一个用于充电高达90kW的电动汽车，第二个用于充电高达350kW的电动汽车。对于PASS+会员，费用分别为0.12美元/分钟和0.24美元/分钟。对于非PASS+会员，费率分别为0.16美元/分钟和0.32美元/分钟。(这再次超过了特斯拉的收费；60kW以下收费0.13美元/分钟，60kW以上收费0.26美元/分钟。)。
电气化美国已经听取了电动汽车车主、潜在客户和长期的行业倡导者的反馈。电气化美国公司总裁兼首席执行官乔瓦尼·帕拉佐(Giovanni Palazzo)表示：因此，我们开发了一种新的价格结构，它是公平、一致的，并认识到客户对千瓦时价格不断增长的需求，“电气化美国”总裁兼首席执行官乔瓦尼·帕拉佐(Giovanni Palazzo)说。

We measure electric vehicle batteries in kWh, so if you're paying to charge an EV, you'd probably expect to be charged by the kWh. And now, if you use an Electrify America charging station in one of 23 states or the District of Columbia, you'll be able to do just that, as the charging company has rolled out a new pricing structure. For the remaining 27 states that require customers to pay by the minute for the electricity they use, the company has simplified its plans and dropped its prices.
 When Electrify America started rolling out the first phase of a $2 billion charging network in 2019,  it did so with a complicated payment structure. When you plug an EV into a fast charger, as part of the handshake process, the car tells the charger the maximum level of power (in kW) it can accept.
 Electrify America used this to determine how much you'd pay, with three different bands (0-75kW, 76-125kW, and 126-350kW), each more expensive than the previous.  And as I discovered, it was quite possible to pay the higher fee even if your car sucked in power at a much lower rate during the charging session—the theoretical maximum kW value is what set the price. (In practice, OEMs like Kia have signed deals with Electrify America so owners get discounted or even free charging for several years.)
 Per-minute pricing for electricity is something that many US states require by law. But 23 of them (and DC) are a little more sensible and only require that customers pay for the amount of electricity used. (Electrify America says these states account for 78 percent of the charging conducted using its network.) For sometime now, Tesla has billed Supercharger users in these states by the kWh, and now Electrify America will do the same. That means you'll pay the same to put a kWh in a Chevy Bolt at 50kW as a Porsche Taycan at 270kW.
 The cost of a kWh for Electrify America's Pass+ members is $0.31/kWh; if you don't want to pay the $4 monthly fee to be a Pass+ member, the rate is $0.43/kWh. This is more expensive than the $0.28/kWh  Tesla currently charges in the same states, although it  no longer offers free Supercharging to new owners.
  In states where Electrify America is required to bill by the minute, it has simplified its structure into just two bands—one for EVs that can charge at up to 90kW, the second for EVs that can charge up to 350kW. For Pass+ members, that will cost $0.12/min and $0.24/min, respectively. For non-Pass+ members, the rates are $0.16/min and $0.32/min. (Again, this is more than Tesla charges; it bills $0.13/min under 60kW, and $0.26/min above 60kW.)
 "Electrify America has listened to feedback from electric vehicle owners, potential customers, and longtime industry advocates. As a result we have developed a new pricing structure that is fair, consistent, and recognizes the increasing customer demand for kilowatt-hour pricing," said Giovanni Palazzo, president and chief executive officer of Electrify America.
title: 电气化美国在23个州改用按千瓦时计费
title_en: Electrify America switches to per-kWh billing in 23 states

note:
最近一项对古代DNA的研究揭示了维京人群体是谁，以及他们是如何与他们遇到的人互动的。从公元前750年到1100年的维京时代，留下了从北美海岸到中亚草原的文化和经济影响，考古显示了几个跨越大陆的文化交流例子。但是，要想了解人们不仅交换思想，而且交换基因的模式，我们需要看看古代人的DNA。
哥本哈根大学基因学家阿肖特·马加里安在接受“每日邮报”采访时表示：“我们非常清楚，一千年前，北欧海盗时代改变了欧洲的文化和政治版图，但我们对伴随这些变化而来的人口结构变化知之甚少。”“基于群体遗传学和方法，这个问题可以很好地解决。”
今天，我们倾向于认为北欧海盗是一大群留着胡须的突袭者，沿着欧洲海岸俯冲，沿着河流逆流而上，横跨北大西洋。但是维京人根本不这样看他们自己。在维京时代出海突袭、贸易、捕鱼和定居的人认为自己是不同群体的成员，拥有共同的文化，但没有共同的身份。事实证明，基因证据对维京人有利。
Margaryan和他的同事最近对生活在公元前2400年到公元前1600年之间的442人的基因组进行了测序。这些人的遗骸在欧洲和格陵兰岛的考古遗址出土，包括斯堪的纳维亚半岛本身。当作者们将这些基因组相互比较，并与数百个已发表的现代人基因组进行比较时，他们发现了细微的差异，将斯堪的纳维亚人分成了四个群体，这与现在生活在瑞典、挪威、丹麦和不列颠群岛的人非常相似。
仔细观察其中一些不同之处，就会为北欧人可能在维京时代开始之前如何四处走动和互动提供了一些线索。考古学家和历史学家仍在争论是什么原因导致几个地方的海员同时开始突袭。这可能是新的人和想法进入斯堪的纳维亚半岛的结果，也可能是由于该地区的动荡和移民造成的。如果不清楚这个时期的人口情况，就很难知道人口迁移是否起到了作用。
我们知道，所有在维京时代从斯堪的纳维亚航海而来的人都是铁器时代(公元前500年至公元800年-不要与开始于20世纪90年代初的瑞典死亡金属时代混淆)的人的后裔。但是基因数据确实表明了一些不同之处。例如，来自瑞典和丹麦的维京时代的人与来自安纳托利亚的新石器时代的农民有更多的共同祖先，他们在大约6000年前向西传播到整个欧洲，比他们的前辈有更多的共同之处。这表明人们和他们的基因从南部和东部流动，越过波罗的海，在北欧海盗时代之前进入瑞典和丹麦。
这本身并不能告诉我们巨大的数量，但像这样的足够多的小拼图最终将足以表明完整的图景可能是什么样子的。
因为Margaryan和他的同事们包括了在北欧海盗时代之前几个世纪生活和死亡的人的基因组，所以可以追踪某些地方的基因多样性是如何随着时间的推移而变化的。其中一些，如瑞典的哥特兰岛和Ӧ岛，自公元1世纪以来就是多样化的社区。但其他地方，如丹麦中部岛屿，在维京时代开始前后经历了基因多样性的突然繁荣，将兰格兰等地拉入了与遥远地方更紧密、更频繁的接触(嗯哼)。
基因证据讲述了与历史和考古学相同的故事：一旦维京时代开始，每个斯堪的纳维亚群体都有自己的贸易路线、联系人、定居点和掠夺地区。从瑞典出发的人大多前往东方；来自挪威的人大多前往西欧和大西洋对岸的爱尔兰、马恩岛，最终到达冰岛和格陵兰岛；来自丹麦的人大多前往英国。
当然，故事总是比看起来复杂一些；玛格丽安和他的同事们在现在的俄罗斯发现了一个丹麦血统的人，在中世纪早期的英格兰，挪威人也在被处决的人之列(可能是因为抢劫)。
Margaryan和他的同事们写道：“很可能许多这样的人来自具有混合祖先的社区，这些社区是由跨越不同文化和大陆的复杂贸易、突袭和定居网络拼凑在一起的。”所有这些遥远地方的人们在维京人和他们的家乡港口留下了持久的基因遗产。
基因组表明，新的遗传物质沿着这些既定的贸易和突袭路线进出斯堪的纳维亚半岛。例如，历史和考古证据告诉我们，挪威人乘船前往不列颠群岛的部分地区，今天挪威人平均有12%到25%的祖先来自这些地方。另一方面，来自瑞典考古遗址的人的基因组更多地是从东欧和中欧人那里获得的祖先。
这种混合更频繁也更快地发生在沿海地区、斯堪的纳维亚半岛南部和大城市。Margaryan和他的同事发现，来自内陆、北部和农村地区的人的基因多样性低于来自更国际化地区的人。这并不令人惊讶，但这表明维京人相当自由地、大规模地与他们在旅行中遇到的人打成一片。他们也愿意欢迎外来者进入他们自己的文化。
Margaryan和他的同事们写道：“我们的结果表明，‘维京人’的身份并不局限于斯堪的纳维亚血统的个人。”毕竟，第一批被测序的皮特兰基因组中有两个来自埋葬在奥克尼斯堪的纳维亚风格坟墓中的人，考古学家也在类似的坟墓中发现了一些其他非斯堪的纳维亚人，其中包括一些显然来自穆斯林国家的人。
note_en:
A recent study of ancient DNA sheds light on who the Viking groups were and how they interacted with the people they met. The Viking Age, from around 750 to 1100 CE, left a cultural and economic impact that stretched from the coast of North America to the Central Asian steppe, and archaeology shows several examples of cultural exchange spanning continents. But to see patterns in how people swapped not only ideas, but genes, we need to look at the DNA of ancient people.
 “We know very well that the Viking Age changed the cultural and political map of Europe a thousand years ago, but we don't really know much about the demographic changes that accompanied these changes,” University of Copenhagen genomicist Ashot Margaryan told Ars. “This can be addressed based on population genetics methods.”
  Today, we tend to think of the Vikings as one big mass of bearded raiders, swooping down European coasts, up rivers, and across the North Atlantic. But the Vikings didn’t see themselves that way at all. The people who set sail to raid, trade, fish, and settle during the Viking Age saw themselves as members of distinct groups, with a shared culture but not a shared identity. The genetic evidence, it turns out, is on the Vikings’ side.
 Margaryan and his colleagues recently sequenced genomes from 442 people who lived between 2400 BCE and 1600 CE. The remains of these people were unearthed at archaeological sites across Europe and Greenland, including Scandinavia itself. When authors compared those genomes to each other and to hundreds of published genomes from modern people, they found subtle differences that sorted Scandinavian people into four groups, which closely resembled people now living in Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and the British Isles.
 A closer look at some of those differences offers some hints about how people in northern Europe may have moved around and interacted just before the start of the Viking Age. Archeologists and historians are still debating what caused seafarers in several places at once to  take up raiding. It could be the result of new people and ideas moving into Scandinavia or due to turmoil and migrations within the region. Without a clear picture of the population from this time, it's difficult to know whether population movements might have contributed.
 We do know that all of the groups of people who sailed from Scandinavia during the Viking Age descended from the people who lived there during the Iron Age (500 BCE to 800 CE—not to be confused with the Swedish Death Metal Age, which started in the early 1990s CE). But the genetic data does suggest a few differences. For instance, Viking Age people from Sweden and Denmark have more ancestry in common with Neolithic farmers from Anatolia, who  spread west across Europe around 6,000 years ago, than their predecessors did. That suggests the flow of people and their genes from the south and east, moving across the Baltic Sea and into Sweden and Denmark just before the Viking Age.
 That doesn’t tell us a tremendous amount on its own, but enough tiny puzzle pieces like this one will eventually be enough to at least suggest what the complete picture might look like.
  Because Margaryan and his colleagues included genomes from people who lived and died centuries before the Viking Age, it’s possible to track how genetic diversity changes over time in certain places. Some, like the Swedish islands of Gotland and Ӧland, had been diverse communities since the first century CE. But others, like the central Danish islands, experienced a sudden flourishing of genetic diversity around the time the Viking Age started, pulling places like Langeland into much closer and more frequent contact (ahem) with distant places.
 The genetic evidence tells the same story as history and archaeology: once the Viking Age began, each of the Scandinavian groups had its own trade routes, contacts, settlements, and raiding areas. People sailing from Sweden mostly went east; people from Norway mostly went to western Europe and across the Atlantic to Ireland, the Isle of Man, and eventually to Iceland and Greenland; people from Denmark mostly went to England.
 Of course, the story is always a little more complex than it seems; Margaryan and his colleagues found a person with Danish ancestry in what’s now Russia, and Norwegians were among people executed (possibly for raiding) in early medieval England.
 “It is likely that many such individuals were from communities with mixed ancestries, thrown together by complex trading, raiding, and settlement networks that crossed cultures and the continent,” wrote Margaryan and his colleagues. And the people of all of those far-flung places left a lasting genetic legacy in the Vikings and their home ports.
 The genomes suggest that new genetic material flowed to and from Scandinavia along those established trade and raiding routes. Historical and archaeological evidence tells us that people from Norway sailed to parts of the British Isles, for example, and today the average Norwegian gets between 12 to 25 percent of their ancestry from those places. The genomes of people from archaeological sites in Sweden, on the other hand, get more of their ancestry from people in eastern and central Europe.
  That mixing happened more often—and sooner—in coastal areas, in southern Scandinavia, and in large cities. Margaryan and his colleagues found less genetic diversity in people from inland, northern, and rural regions than in people from more cosmopolitan places. That’s not surprising, but it indicates that Vikings mingled pretty freely, and on a large scale, with the people they encountered on their travels. They were also willing to welcome outsiders into their own culture.
 “Our results show that ‘Viking’ identity was not limited to individuals of Scandinavian descent,” wrote Margaryan and his colleagues. Two of the first Pictish genomes ever sequenced came from people buried in Scandinavian-style graves in Orkney, after all, and archaeologists have found a number of other non-Scandinavians buried in similar graves, including some who apparently originally came from Muslim countries.
title: 古代DNA揭示了维京人的起源，旅行
title_en: Ancient DNA sheds light on Viking origins, travels

对一些人来说，总结NVIDIA RTX 3080的不同之处可能围绕着一系列高端(有时是专有)技术。光线追踪。深度学习超级采样。超快的内存带宽。在某些情况下，NVIDIA售价699美元的新GPU在软件利用其非常特殊的特权和功能时处于最佳状态。
但众所周知，2018&#39；的RTX 2000图形处理器系列给粉丝留下的高价选择并没有提供足够的普遍令人垂涎三尺的提振。当时，我把这些卡称为RTX彩票的入场券，在将近两年的时间里，我几乎没有收到什么回报。
它正在变得更好，特别是DirectX12旗舰版和一对下一代游戏机终于享受到了2018年腾讯通的大部分特权。AMD很快就会用它自己强大的rDNA2图形处理器系列在这方面大赚一笔。一旦NVIDIA的RTX功能感觉不那么专有，我们应该会在更多的游戏中看到它们--但这也让NVIDIA不得不回答一个问题。那你还有什么发现？
这就引出了我更喜欢总结RTX 3080不同之处的方式，正如我的ARS同事Lee Hutchinson所提示的那样。我几乎可以听到他在ARS聊天会话中关于我的GPU测试的急切喘息声，当时他输入了四个词：&#34；VR中的精英危险？&#34；
(Ars Technica可能会通过会员计划从本帖子上的链接获得销售补偿。)。
截至发稿时，这款太空探索游戏的PC版并未针对DirectX12旗舰版或RTX特定功能进行优化。这只是一场设定在无限宇宙中的饥饿游戏--在这场游戏中，你可以在荒芜的空间中漂浮片刻，然后突然扭曲成史诗般的、旋转的全能混战，旁边是一个几何密度很高的队形。
这是很棒的东西。它在虚拟现实中表现得更好。当你可以翻转像Valve Index这样的高端耳机上的120 Hz开关，把双手放在你最喜欢的HOTAS钻机上，对任何胆敢打断你的货物洗牌任务的人进行零重力，360度地狱火的时候，这种模式甚至会更好。所有这些都不会丢失帧。(由于这款游戏强调高速旋转，正常的90 Hz虚拟现实标准对标准问题的胃来说是不够的。游戏中的帧越多，就意味着更舒适。)。
对于像哈钦森这样的人来说，他的游戏设备有很高的价格上限，但不是无限的，我可以这样说：有了RTX 3080，你可以在VR预设的最高电压下运行Elite Dangge，在Valve Index中切换到120 Hz模式，预计会有几乎锁定的帧率。Fallout 4 VR也是如此，这是对贝塞斯达的RPG进行了残酷的未经优化的VR转换，我最终只能以90fps的锁定速度运行(或者徘徊在Valve Index上100-110fps的可变区域)。虚拟现实接口推出三年后，我实际上也想这么玩。
对于这张卡所处理的要求如此之高的PC软件，我可以说类似的话，这是我无法用2018年令人眼花缭乱的1199美元RTX 2080 Ti的方式。在另一个世界-NVIDIA在GPU竞赛中没有对手，多年来击败了AMD-NVIDIA本可以继续对2018年的卡收取800或900美元左右的费用，它的价格更多的是关于专用的效果处理核心，而不是纯粹的、全面的能力。
随着AMD终于在今年紧随其后，NVIDIA推出了一款售价699美元的RTX 3080，其Founders Edition没有添加任何超频，在现代3D游戏和软件中一直击败超频2080 Ti Founders Edition(并在RTX特有的功能上展示了更强大的肌肉)。在性能测试中，获得的性能边际总是超过13%的门槛，有时甚至更高。(就“精英危险虚拟现实”(Elite Dangge VR；s；HIGH)预设而言，这种预设的基准难度要大得多，但它在整个画面时间上的差异约为20%。)。
对于世界上疯狂的李·哈钦森来说，这不仅仅是个好消息。这张卡对市场的影响将有望将平均GPU价值主张推向合理的领域。699美元的GPU可能不是你喜欢的，但如果RTX 3080(以及它的兄弟机型RTX 3070，定于10月份推出，售价为499美元)以下的所有产品的价格根据以下基准进行调整，这意味着坚如磐石的1080p或1440p GPU最终可能会在你的预算范围内。
note_en:
For some people, summarizing the  Nvidia RTX 3080 difference may revolve around a slew of high-end—and sometimes proprietary—technologies. Ray tracing. Deep-learning super sampling. Crazy-fast memory bandwidth. In some cases, Nvidia's new $699 GPU is at its best when software leverages its very specific perks and features.
 But as we all know,  2018's line of RTX 2000 GPUs left fans with high-priced options that didn't offer enough universally drool-worthy boosts. At the time, I called those cards "a ticket to the RTX lottery," and for nearly two years, the payoff was scant.
 It's getting better—especially as DirectX 12 Ultimate and a pair of next-gen consoles finally lean into most of 2018's RTX-specific perks. AMD will soon cash in on this stuff with its own powerful RDNA 2 line of GPUs. Once Nvidia's RTX features feel less "proprietary," we should see them in more games—but that also leaves Nvidia to answer a question.  Well, what else ya got?
 This brings me to the way I prefer to summarize the RTX 3080 difference, as prompted by my Ars colleague Lee Hutchinson. I could practically hear the eager gasps through his keystrokes in an Ars chat session about my GPU tests when he typed four words: "  Elite Dangerous in VR?"
  (Ars Technica may earn compensation for sales from links on this post through  affiliate programs.)
As of press time, this space-exploration game's PC version is not optimized for DirectX 12 Ultimate or RTX-specific features. It's just a damned hungry game set in an infinite universe—one where you can float through barren space one moment, then abruptly warp into an epic, spinning-all-around dogfight next to a geometrically dense formation.
 It's awesome stuff. It's even better in VR. And that mode is  even better when you can  flip the 120Hz switch on a high-end headset like the Valve Index, put both hands on your favorite HOTAS rig, and lay zero-G, 360-degree hellfire upon anyone who might dare interrupt your cargo-shuffling mission... all without dropping frames. (Thanks to the game's emphasis on high-speed rotation, the normal 90Hz VR standard isn't enough for standard-issue stomachs. More frames in that game mean more comfort.)
 To someone like Mr. Hutchinson, who has a high price ceiling for his gaming rig but not an infinite one, I can say this: with the RTX 3080, you can run  Elite Dangerous at its "high" preset in VR, flip to 120Hz mode in Valve Index, and expect a nearly locked framerate. The same goes for   Fallout 4 VR, a brutally unoptimized VR conversion of Bethesda's RPG, which I can  finally run at a locked 90fps (or hover in variable 100-110fps territory on Valve Index). Three years after that VR port's launch, I actually want to play it that way.
   I can say similar things for so much demanding PC software as handled by this card in ways that I couldn't with 2018's eye-watering $1,199 RTX 2080 Ti. In an alternate universe—the one where Nvidia ran unopposed in the GPU race, beating the pants off AMD for years—Nvidia could have continued charging something like $800 or $900 for that 2018 card, whose price tag was much more about dedicated effects-processing cores than about sheer, across-the-board power.
 With AMD finally nipping on its heels this year, Nvidia has arrived with a $699 beast of an RTX 3080 whose "Founders Edition," without any overclocks added, consistently beats an overclocked 2080 Ti Founders Edition in modern 3D games and software (and flexes stronger muscles on RTX-specific features). In performance tests, the margin of gained performance always clears the 13-percent threshold, sometimes much more. (In the case of  Elite Dangerous VR's "high" preset, which is much tougher to benchmark, it makes a roughly 20 percent difference in frame times across the board.)
 This isn't just good news for the crazy Lee Hutchinsons of the world. This card's impact on the market will  hopefully push the average GPU value proposition into reasonable territory. A $699 GPU may not be your cup of tea, but if prices for everything beneath the RTX 3080 (and its sibling,  the RTX 3070, slated to launch in October at $499) adjust according to the below benchmarks, that means a rock-solid 1080p or 1440p GPU may finally land within your budgetary reach.
title: NVIDIA RTX 3080评论：4K卓越，售价699美元-对于更便宜的GPU来说是个好消息
title_en: Nvidia RTX 3080 review: 4K greatness at $699—and good news for cheaper GPUs

据《华尔街日报》周二报道，美国联邦贸易委员会(Federal Trade Commission)的官员已经开始针对Facebook展开潜在的法律诉讼，这是该机构对该公司长达一年的反垄断调查的一部分。
据《华尔街日报》报道，联邦贸易委员会尚未就是否最终起诉Facebook做出决定。为可能的诉讼准备法律论据通常是调查的一部分，但也表明该机构的调查可能已接近尾声。
Facebook拒绝置评，联邦贸易委员会也没有立即回复对这篇报道的置评请求。
去年7月，Facebook在一份收益报告中披露，它正在接受联邦贸易委员会的调查，而与此同时，司法部和国会议员正在进行反垄断调查。
那年夏天晚些时候，“华尔街日报”(The Wall Street Journal)报道称，联邦贸易委员会对Facebook过去的收购特别感兴趣，以及它进行这些收购是否是为了防范潜在的竞争，并一直在与被Facebook收购的几家公司的创始人交谈。
在今年7月的国会反垄断听证会上，这些交易成为议员们关注的焦点，他们就Facebook收购Instagram和WhatsApp一事盘问了首席执行官马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)，并公布了一些文件，披露了一些竞争对手担心如果不出售，他们是如何被Facebook压垮的。
Politico上个月报道称，联邦贸易委员会还为调查听取了扎克伯格的证词，此前该机构就侵犯隐私的指控对这家社交媒体巨头进行调查时拒绝这样做，最终对Facebook处以50亿美元的罚款。
作为目前调查的一部分，FTC可能会寻求一系列选择，包括迫使其解除过去的合并，限制其开展业务的方式，或者对其处以罚款，尽管该机构首先需要在法庭上证明自己的案件，或者与Facebook达成和解。

note_en:
Officials at the Federal Trade Commission have started building a potential legal case against Facebook as part of the agency's year-long antitrust investigation into the company,  The Wall Street Journal reported Tuesday.
 The FTC has not yet made a decision on whether it will ultimately sue Facebook, according to The Wall Street Journal. Preparing legal arguments for a possible lawsuit is often part of investigations, but also signals that the agency may be nearing the end of its probe.
 Facebook declined to comment, and the FTC did not immediately reply to a request for comment on this story.
 In July of last year, Facebook disclosed in an earnings report that it was  under investigation by the FTC, which came amid existing antitrust probes from the Department of Justice and members of Congress.
   The Wall Street Journal reported later that summer that the FTC was specifically interested in  Facebook's past acquisitions and whether it made those purchases to head off potential competition, and had been speaking to the founders of several companies bought up by Facebook.
 During a  congressional antitrust hearing this July, those deals became the focus for lawmakers, who grilled CEO Mark Zuckerberg about Facebook's acquisitions of Instagram and WhatsApp and published documents revealing how some competitors  feared being crushed by Facebook if they didn't sell.
 Politico reported last month that  the FTC also took testimony from Zuckerberg for its investigation, something the agency declined to do when it previously investigated the social media giant over allegations of privacy violations, for which ultimately fined Facebook $5 billion.
 The FTC could pursue a range of options as part of its current probe, including forcing it to unwind past mergers, placing restrictions on how it does business, or fining it, though the agency would first need to prove its case in court or reach a settlement with Facebook.
